version_description: Neat formatting, all lyrics with accompanying chords, additional playing information, full chord legend included https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iLtGTwIxZZs.
tuning: E A D G B E
content:
Song: I'm on fire
Artist: Bruce Springsteen
Composer: Bruce Springsteen
Album: Born in the U.S.A.

Tabbed by: DJ   8th Mar 2015

Here's a link to a live acoustic version,
a semi-tone down from the album one, in Eb:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_jZxAWdfNp4

NOTE: In the live video Bruce plays with the Capo
at the 3rd fret, so the song is a semi-tone lower
than the original studio version. He also throws
in random embellishments with a Csus4 (x33010),
occasionally a Asus2 (x02200) and sometimes adds a
passing B note (x2xxxx) between the Am and C chords
in the Intro, Break and Coda.

I would suggest using the voicings in the text
column numbers below.
Regarding the CHORD BOX DIAGRAMS please follow the
information at the bottom of the tab.

CAPO: 4th fret 
(Will then sound in the original recorded key of E)

Chord names & fret positions relative to the capo:
C = x32010  Am = x02210  F = 133211  G = 320033

Please take a second to scroll down to the bottom of
the page and select a star rating for my tab, thanks
====================================================

[tab]      [ch]F[/ch]                    [ch]Am[/ch]

[Verse 2]

Tell me now, baby, is he good to you?[/tab]

[tab]     [ch]F[/ch]                   [ch]Am[/ch]
Oh - woh, I can take you higher,[/tab]

Sometimes, it's like someone took a knife,[/tab]

Baby, edgy and dull, and cut a six-inch valley...[/tab]
[tab]               [ch]Am[/ch]
...Through the middle of my skull[/tab]

[Verse 3]
[tab]   [ch]C[/ch]
At night, I wake up, with the sheets soaking wet,[/tab]
[tab]      [ch]C[/ch]
And a freight train running...[/tab]
[tab]               [ch]C[/ch]
...Through the middle of my head,[/tab]
[tab]     [ch]F[/ch]                        [ch]Am[/ch]
Only you  ...can cool my de - sire,[/tab]

Woo,  hoo---, hoo!   Woo - oo, hoo - hoo![/tab]
====================================================
As a very subtle alternative to the basic chords
in the version below I've indicated chords suggested
by a few additional notes that the keyboards add
to the arrangement, so for a single acoustic guitar
they may be a slightly more interesting option than
just the long stretches of C and Am in the tab above

Chord names & fret positions are relative to the capo:
[ch]Cadd9[/ch] = x32030   [ch]Am7[/ch]   = x02213   [ch]C[/ch] *   = x32013
[ch]Am[/ch]    = x02210   [ch]Asus4[/ch] = x02230

[Alternative Intro x2]
[tab]  [ch]Cadd9[/ch]
| /  /  /  / | /  /  /  / | /  /  /  / | /  /  /  / |[/tab]
[tab]  [ch]Am7[/ch] [ch]Am[/ch]
| /  /  /  / | /  /  /  / | /  /  /  / | /  /  /  / |[/tab]

| /  /  /  / | /  /  /  / | /  /  /  / | /  /  /  / |[/tab]
[tab]  [ch]Cadd9[/ch]
| /  /  /  / | /  /  /  / | /  /  /  / | /  /  /  / |[/tab]
[tab]  [ch]Asus4[/ch]                                          [ch]Am[/ch]

[tab]  [ch]C[/ch] *

=====================================================
For the GUITAR chord box diagrams to match my
suggested choices you need to manually select the
following choice of options by clicking on the
forward/backward arrows below four of the diagrams.
The other boxes should be the default first option
and don't need any reselecting. 
As long as you are logged into your account, these
choices will be saved automatically for every time
you view this tab.
   G          Cadd9        Am7        Asus4
